feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Extended Warehouse Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Extended Warehouse Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Resource Handover for Product WTs in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

I would like to ask for your help and technical guidance on how to manage a resource handover within a single 2-step Product Warehouse Task (Replenishment) in S/4HANA Cloud, public edition.

Process Facts:

  • Context: EWM Stock Replenishment between two buildings.
  • Execution: Due to the physical distance, the process is split between two employees. Resource A performs the source confirmation (picking) in Building 1. Resource B performs the destination confirmation (putaway) in Building 2.
  • System Environment: Product-based WTs (no HUs used). Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) is not implemented.
  • Current Issue: After Resource A confirms the source data, the Warehouse Order remains assigned to Resource A. When Resource B attempts to confirm the destination, the system prevents it (Error /SCWM/RF_EN140).

Questions:

  1. What is the standard procedure to release the resource assignment after the first step (source confirmation) so a second resource can take over and confirm the destination? (I have even tried in Warehouse Monitor to unassign the old resource and then assign a new one, but it still says that the old res. is processing the task)
  2. Is there a standard Exception Code available in Public Cloud to trigger this handover for Product WTs?
  3. How can the "Stock on Resource" be transferred from Resource A to Resource B within the same Warehouse Task?

Best regards,

Adam Toth

entry.title: How to create WTs in EWM without a batch?
entry.content:
Hello,

We work with a Storage Type that stores multiple batches in the same storage bin.

When creating the picking WTs, system determines a specific batch to be created.
However, in this multi batch ST, warehouse workers would take the lot that is closest to them, to avoid downtime from having to move all the pallets to look for a particular lot that is filled in the WT.

So, how can we create picking WTs without a predetermined batch?

If you can make a recommendation to improve this in operation, it is also welcome.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: PU Withdrawn qty exceeded by 1 EA : 700000367 YES1
entry.content:
Hi All
i am getting an issue while posting the GR from EWM to ERP PU Withdrawn qty exceeded by 1 EA : 700000367 YES1 ROD
In my Case PO QTY EQ 1 & GR QTY EQ 1But still i was getting qty exceeded issue can you please help me to resolve this error

entry.title: Error Work center
entry.content:
Can anyone help in this error? How to solve this

 

Thanks,

Deepa

entry.title: Cant we configure delete stock identification , remove only delete identification on storage type
entry.content:
Can't we configure delete stock identification, remove only delete identification on storage type.

its troughing error it allowing only final storage type.

Kindly help me to assist for this issue

Thanks and Regards,

Anjanna

entry.title: Spool is not generating for the parent HU
entry.content:
Hi All

From the Tcode:/SCWM/PACK, when one of the child handling unit is removed from the Parent HU, spool is not generating for the Parent HU for printing the Parent HU with existing data or qty.

But if we add the child HU to the existing Parent HU, spool is getting generated.

Condition record with HU step-U is maintained for the parent HU (Pallet).

Requesting to suggest if any thing is missing.

 

Thanks & Regards

Chandra

entry.title: Unable to Change Posting Date for Physical Inventory in Difference Analyzer - Embedded EWM
entry.content:
Currently it is impossible to set or change the posting date for Physical Inventory Documents. You can define the document date and the counting date, but none of them impact the posting date of the PI document. Whenever we post the difference to ERP side "Analyze Difference" the posting date field is filled with the current date and is unchangeable. 

--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  1. Create a PI document in /SCWM/PI_CREATE
  2. Go to edit mode on PI document and do the "Count" on back date and "Post" the count in EWM and save.
  3. Open the Difference Analyzer app /SCWM/DIFF_ANALYZER
  4. Unable to change the posting date

How to influence the posting date in ERP?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: IM-WM stock difference
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

We have negative stock in warehouse management. Is it okay to manually update the LQUA table for the data shown below?

Total Stock – GESME
Available Stock – VERME

In Inventory Management, I have 20 in stock. For example, if Total Stock is -40, I will change it to -20 and then use LB11 (manual TO creation) to cover it with inventory stock.

 

BR,

Aygun Aghayeva

#WarehouseManagemant #LX23 #LB11 #WM

entry.title: Determination of printer for printing handling units from the condition record in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi Team

Need your inputs for the below requirement.

In the current design,Pallet labels is getting   printed out using network printers. Handling unit is created by 3rd party tool on BTP application and when HU is created in SAP ,will trigger the PPF .When PPF is triggered BADI will suppress the standard step and returns back the PDF preview back to the UI screen ,where the user will select the network printer for printing the HU.

Note: We had not maintained the Storage type & bin details in the workcenter master data.Its Blank.Created storage type as work center and maintained it in the condition record.

Fields in the condition record: Handling Unit Type,Warehouse Number,HU Processing Step,Packaging Material,Work Center,Form,Printer Name

In the New Design,Planning to maintain the printer in SAP, so that when user clicks on the print button on the BTP screen, it will physically print out the HU label to the assigned printer in the condition record.

Concern is ,in the condition record ,HU type and pack material is same .If we maintain the condition record for one printer ,can't maintain the same set of details to other printer.Only field that can be used is work center.But we had not maintained the storage type details in the workcenter master data.

Is there any alternative solution to determine the printer .Please suggest.

Thanks & Regards

Chandra

entry.title: SAP Public cloud WM - Outbound pick task is not picking the destination BIN S920
entry.content:
Hello team,

Could you please tell me which config i am missing from starter system , simple ask over here. During picking system is not finding staging storage type as well picking bin

entry.title: Error "/SCWM/L3690: Enter a destination HU" during RF Putaway WT Confirmation
entry.content:
We are encountering a discrepancy in RF Putaway behavior between the standard Starter Warehouse and a custom Warehouse within the same Starter System.

1. Symptom When performing RF Warehouse Order (WO) Putaway to a storage bin in Warehouse (Storage Type S001), which was provided by the Starter System, the following error occurs:

  • Error Message: Enter a destination HU (Message ID: /SCWM/L3690)

2. Comparison In the same Starter System environment, we created a new custom Warehouse (Storage Type ZRM01). When performing RF Putaway in this warehouse, the error does not occur, and the process is completed successfully without a destination HU.

There is no difference in the settings between the two storage types. I am curious which specific configuration in the standard warehouse is triggering this error.

entry.title: The function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 does not exist in the system S/4 release 2023
entry.content:
Hi

We have upgraded to HANA 2023 and we had used the function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 in some of our programs.  As per SAP note 3411865, The function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 does not exist in the system S/4 release 2023. We have checked for any replacement FM but didn't find any. This note is also not implementable. Kindly provide an alternate FM as we need this functionality for some of our developments

 

Regards

Vijay

entry.title: Destination bin requires handling unit /scwm/rf_en223
entry.content:
Hola Gurú

Tengo un problema, a continuación se detallan los siguientes puntos:

Al recoger el material con su unidad de manipulación, para su reposición se muestra el siguiente error:

El contenedor de destino requiere la unidad de manipulación  scwm rf_en223

¿Podrías ayudarme, por favor?

Saludos

Marcelo

entry.title: WT confirmation via /SCWM/RFUI does not trigger Suitable enhancement / BADI after posting
entry.content:
The ask is to trigger a web service automatically when a Warehouse Task is confirmed and posted to the database for a particular warehouse num (LGNUM).

Analysis Performed:Debugged RFUI WT confirmation flow Traced execution through function modules and classes Verified that: No relevant BAdI (CALL BADI) is triggered during RF confirmation.

 Checked potential function module: /SCWM/TO_CONFIRM Identified as a possible common posting point, but not confirmed as enhancement-safeQuestion to SAP:

  1. What is the recommended enhancement point (BAdI/User Exit) to trigger custom logic after WT confirmation that works for both: /SCWM/TO_CONF and /SCWM/RFUI 
  2. Is there any standard BAdI or enhancement spot available at the common posting layer of WT confirmation?
  3. Can /SCWM/TO_CONFIRM be safely enhanced for this purpose, or is there a standard alternative?

entry.title: Batch number creation should not dictate EWM distribution
entry.content:
Hello team,

 

I have created two ASN.

180000050 – Batch number I did not assign during ASN creation

180000049 – Batch number I have assigned during ASN creation

 

But Batch number creation should not dictate which IBD will be distributed or which IBD should not be disturbed in WM public cloud

entry.title: Automatic picking of consignement stock
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation with WM, and I would like to confirm the standard behavior regarding automatic picking from consignment stock.

Context

  • Outbound process with automatic warehouse task creation from an outbound delivery
  • Product available in a single storage bin
  • The bin contains:
    • Own stock (non‑consignment)
    • Consignment stock
  • Both stock types are visible at bin level and can be picked manually without any issue

Observed behavior

  • During automatic warehouse task creation, the system:
    • Picks the own stock first from the bin
    • Once the own stock is depleted, the bin is ignored, even though consignment stock is still physically available
    • The system then searches another bin with own stock, or no WT is created
  • However, during manual picking, the user can explicitly select and pick the consignment stock from the same bin

Question

Is this behavior standard by design in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud WM?

More specifically:

  • Is consignment stock intentionally excluded from automatic stock removal / automatic WT creation?
  • Is automatic picking from consignment stock not supported unless a manual selection or a prior posting change (consignment → own stock) is performed?
  • Are there any standard configuration options in Public Cloud WM to allow automatic picking of consignment stock, or is this intentionally restricted to manual processes only?

The behavior looks consistent and logical from a valuation and control perspective, but I would like to confirm whether this is:

  • a standard limitation,
  • or if there is an official recommendation / design decision documented by SAP.

Thank you in advance for your insights.

entry.title: Picking sequence: Pick, pack, pass
entry.content:
Hi SAP EWM Community,

we use Pick, Pack, Pass in the warehouse and essentially have three activity areas:

Activity Area A
Activity Area B
Activity Area C

They are joined under 1 Higher Level Activity Area ABC. 

For production supply, the optimal picking sequence is A → B → C as Production is next to C. For distribution, our optimal sequence would be C → B → A as Loading Area is next to A. 

We haven’t yet found a way to change the picking sequence based on the process, e.g., warehouse process type.

Is this possible within SAP Standard ? Does anyone have an idea?

Thank you in advance,
Nicolas

entry.title: How to dynamically determine Warehouse Source Storage Type by Customer for the same material code
entry.content:
Hi

A single material code is being sold to multiple customers (A, B, C).

We want to store and pick this material from separate Storage Types assigned to each customer (e.g., ST_A, ST_B, ST_C).

However, only one Stock Removal Control Indicator can be set in the Material Master.

In this case, is there a way to dynamically determine the source storage type and bin when a Warehouse Order is created based on an Outbound Delivery?

For example, can we ensure that the source storage type and bin are determined as "ST_A" for Customer A, and "ST_B" for Customer B?

entry.title: PMR staging
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

While creating staging warehouse task in -/SCWM/STAGE

There are 5 components in production order that needs to be stage

Out of 5 components ,4 Components is having stock and for one component stock is not present.

When I’m trying to create stating warehouse task in /SCWM/STAGE with reference to production order system is not creating WT for components with available qty

So businesses expectation is that it should create WT for components with available stock qty

 

Thanks,

Virsinh Jadhav
